title: The Seq2Seq Bottleneck
tagline: Watch translation quality collapse as source sentences grow
summary: Seq2Seq compresses every source token into a single context vector, then generates the target from it. Type a source sentence, slide its length, and watch the context vector's 'information density' fall off. This is the exact failure mode that made attention (1 year later) inevitable.
highlights: Type a source sentence; see it encoded token by token into a fixed-size context.
Extend the source — watch the fixed context's 'saturation' rise.
Toggle context dimension (64 / 256 / 512) and see the bend-point in quality.
Read the 2014 performance curves — BLEU drops ~30% past 30 tokens on vanilla Seq2Seq.
Preview: attention lets the decoder peek at ALL encoder states, sidestepping the bottleneck entirely.
